From Reno eastward, the poles increase in height until near Columbus, Ohio, which is about four-fifths of the way across the nation. At Columbus, the flags are about 10 feet high. 4. Then, as approaching the eastern terminus (New York), the poles suddenly increase in height dramatically. The last 75 miles are taken by firms with sales over $500k, last 50 miles by those over $1 million, and so on, with the largest companies near the end in New York. So, the road goes from San Francisco (west) to New York (east). The places mentioned along the route are San Francisco, Reno, Columbus, and New York. The question asks for the order from east to west. So, starting from the easternmost point and moving westward. Let me break down the positions: - San Francisco is the starting point (west coast, California). - Reno is mentioned first after San Francisco; so San Francisco → Reno. - Then from Reno eastward to Columbus, Ohio. Columbus is about four-fifths of the way across the nation. Since the entire road is from SF to NY, Columbus is somewhere in the middle to the east. - Finally, the road ends in New York (east coast). Therefore, the order from west to east is: San Francisco → Reno → Columbus → New York. But the question asks for the order from east to west. So reverse that sequence. East to west would be New York (eastmost), then Columbus, then Reno, then San Francisco (westmost). Looking at the options: Option C: New York, Columbus, Reno, San Francisco.
